Transfer students who do not take EEC 010 will substitute four additional units of upper division electives post-matriculation.</p>","groupId":"22b37645-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":7,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"<p>This is a competitive major and is subject to the <a href=\"https://www.ucdavis.edu/admissions/undergraduate/transfer/selective-major-requirements\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\">Selective Major Review</a> process. Students need to take all courses for a letter grade with no grade less than C and complete all major preparatory course requirements by the end of spring term prior to their fall enrollment. All students need to meet a minimum <strong>3.10 GPA </strong>cumulative <u>and</u> a <strong>3.20 GPA</strong> in major preparation coursework.</p><p><br></p><p>All majors in the College of Engineering are selective and have additional transfer requirements.</p><p><br></p><p>Important Information:</p><p><br></p><p>Complete the courses below:</p><p>●    Chemistry (CHE) 002A</p><p>●    Engineering (ENG) 017</p><p>●    Electrical Computer Engineering (EEC) 007</p><p>●    Mathematics (MAT) 021A/B/C/D</p><p>●    Mathematics (MAT) 022A/B</p><p>●    Physics (PHY) 009A/B/C/D</p><p><br></p><p>Visit Department agreements as needed</p><p><br></p><p>Contact your UC Davis Advisor for questions (<a href=\"https://ua.ucdavis.edu/portal/find-my-advisor\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\">Find my advisor</a>)</p><p><br></p><p>Note:</p><p><strong>Advanced Placement (AP) and International Baccalaureate (IB) Examination:</strong></p><p>AP and IB examination credit policies are detailed in the UC Davis <a href=\"https://local-resources.ucdavis.edu/local_resources/docs/catalog/GenCat20252026.pdf\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\">General Catalog</a>. Quick reference charts for <a href=\"https://local-resources.ucdavis.edu/local_resources/docs/APIBCharts/APCurrent.pdf\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\">AP</a> and <a href=\"https://local-resources.ucdavis.edu/local_resources/docs/APIBCharts/IBCurrent.pdf\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\">IB</a>.</p>","groupId":"36b37645-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":3,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"<p>At the time of TAG submission, the College of Engineering requires a minimum <strong>3.50 </strong>cumulative GPA and at least a <strong>3.50</strong> GPA across the entire group of courses with no grades less than a C to receive a Transfer Admission Guarantee.</p>","groupId":"38b37645-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":5,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"<p>The <a href=\"https://local-resources.ucdavis.edu/local_resources/docs/catalog/GenCat20252026.pdf\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\">Electrical Engineering</a> Bachelor of Science program is accredited by the Engineering Accreditation Commission of <a href=\"http://www.abet.org\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\">ABET</a> under the commission's General Criteria and Program Criteria for Electrical, Computer, Communications, Telecommunication(s), and Similarly Named Engineering Programs.</p><p><br></p><p>Electrical engineering involves the design, analysis, and effective use of electrical systems including electronic computers. Electrical systems and computers play a central role in nearly all aspects of modern life, including communication, medicine, education, environmental protection, space exploration, defense, and home entertainment.</p><p><br></p><p>Students who complete the Electrical Engineering curriculum will obtain a Bachelor of Science in Electrical Engineering, one of the engineering degrees recognized in all fifty states as eligible for registration as a Professional Engineer.</p><p><br></p><p>The Electrical & Computer Engineering program educational objectives have been developed to address the needs of our constituencies. The objectives of the Electrical & Computer Engineering programs are as follow:</p><p><br></p><ul><li>Graduates create value for their employers, demonstrating knowledge and initiative and making beneficial contributions beyond the workplace. This can also result in patents, awards, publications and presentations.</li></ul><p><br></p><ul><li>Graduates grow their capabilities through advanced education and professional development.</li></ul><p><br></p><ul><li>Graduates provide leadership and be proactive in their profession and/or communities.</li></ul>","groupId":"41b37645-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":1,"area":"General"},{"type":"RequirementTitle","content":"ENGINEERING","groupId":"c5b7a92b-e51e-47ea-b52c-019867fd8607","position":19,"area":"Requirements"},{"type":"RequirementGroup","hideSectionLetters":false,"sections":[{"type":"Section","rows":[{"position":0,"cells":[{"type":"Course","course":{"courseIdentifierParentId":260112,"courseTitle":"Engineering
